dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3459 IS: Support Small Business Growth Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Ossoff (for himself and Mrs. Hyde-Smith) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to provide a payroll tax deduction for certain small businesses, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Support Small Business Growth Act of 2025.

section.header: Payroll tax deduction for small businesses

subsection.text: Part VI of subchapter B of chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by inserting after section 176 the following new section:

section.header: Payroll deduction for qualified small businesses

subsection.text: In the case of a qualified small business, in addition to the deduction allowed by section 162(a)(1), there shall be allowed as a deduction an amount equal to the sum of the applicable amounts determined under subsection (b)(2) with respect to each full-time employee of the employer which has been designated by such employer pursuant to subsection (b)(1).

subparagraph.text: For purposes of subsection (a), with respect to any taxable year, the full-time employees designated by the employer (at such time and in such manner as the Secretary may provide) shall—

clause.text: not exceed the applicable maximum number of employees,
only include those full-time employees with the lowest wages paid during such taxable year relative to all other full-time employees of such employer, and
not include any highly compensated employee.

subparagraph.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the applicable maximum number with respect to any qualified small business is—

clause.text: 10, in the case of a taxable year beginning in 2026, 2027, 2028, 2029, or 2030,
8, in the case of a taxable year beginning in 2031,
6, in the case of a taxable year beginning in 2032,
4, in the case of a taxable year beginning in 2033, and

subparagraph.text: For purposes of subsection (a), with respect to each full-time employee designated pursuant to paragraph (1), the applicable amount shall be an amount equal to the lesser of—

clause.text: 12 percent of the wages paid to such employee during the taxable year.

subparagraph.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the applicable wage limitation is—

clause.text: $8,000, with respect to the number of full-time employees designated pursuant to paragraph (1) which is equal to—

subclause.text: the applicable maximum number with respect to the qualified small business for the taxable year, reduced by

clause.text: $6,000, with respect to 1 additional such employee, if applicable, and
$4,000, with respect to 1 additional such employee, if applicable.

subsection.text: For purposes of this section, the term qualified small business means, with respect to a taxable year, a small business concern (as defined under section 3 of the Small Business Act) which—

paragraph.text: as of the last date of such taxable year—

subparagraph.text: has not more than 15 full-time employees, and
meets the gross receipts test of section 448(c), and

paragraph.text: certifies to the Secretary, at such time and in such manner as the Secretary shall prescribe, that such concern meets the requirements of this subsection for the taxable year.
The term full-time employee has the meaning given such term by section 4980H(c)(4).
The term highly compensated employee has the meaning given such term by section 414(q).

subsection.text: This section shall not apply to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2033.
The table of sections for part VI of subchapter B of chapter 1 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by inserting after the item relating to section 176 the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 177. Payroll deduction for qualified small businesses.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025.
